meta_has_cta.meaning: CTA means Call-to-Action — a short line that tells the visitor what to do next, like 'Shop Now' or 'Free Shipping'. In meta description, it appears under your title on Google.
meta_has_cta.ok: Your meta description encourages clicks with a clear action (Shop Now, Free shipping, etc.).
meta_has_cta.fail: Google shows your page with plain text — no reason for shoppers to click from search results.
meta_has_cta.fix: Add one action line in meta description, e.g. "Shop {product_name} today — Free shipping on orders above ₹999."

headings_logical_hierarchy.meaning: Headings should follow order: one H1 (main title), then H2 (sections), then H3 (sub-sections). Skipping levels (H1 then H3) confuses Google and readers.
headings_logical_hierarchy.ok: Headings follow a clear structure — easy for Google and customers to scan.
headings_logical_hierarchy.fail: Headings jump levels or are out of order — search engines may not understand page structure.
headings_logical_hierarchy.fix: Use one H1 (product name), then H2 for sections (Description, Reviews, FAQ), then H3 for sub-points.

headings_keywords.meaning: Your target search words (e.g. your main product category) should appear naturally in H2/H3 section headings — not stuffed, but clearly present.
headings_keywords.ok: Your main search terms appear in H2/H3 headings — helps ranking for those queries.
headings_keywords.fail: Headings don't include your target keywords — you miss SEO signals in subheadings.
headings_keywords.fix: Add natural keyword phrases in section headings, e.g. "{product_name} — Details & Features".

kw_in_title.meaning: The title tag is the blue clickable headline on Google. Your main keyword should appear here so Google knows what the page is about.
kw_in_title.ok: Your main keyword appears in the page title shown on Google.
kw_in_title.fail: Title tag doesn't include your primary keyword — harder to rank for that search term.
kw_in_title.fix: Put brand + primary keyword first in title, e.g. "{brand} | {product_name}".

kw_in_h1.meaning: H1 is the biggest heading visible on the page. It should include your primary keyword because Google treats it as the main topic of the page.
kw_in_h1.ok: The main heading on the page includes your target keyword.
kw_in_h1.fail: H1 doesn't match what people search for — Google may not know this page's main topic.
kw_in_h1.fix: Make H1 the product name + key attribute, e.g. "{product_name}".

kw_in_meta.meaning: Meta description is the 1–2 line summary under your title on Google. Including your keyword here helps relevance and click-through.
kw_in_meta.ok: Meta description includes your target keyword — reinforces relevance in search snippets.
kw_in_meta.fail: Meta description missing your main keyword — snippet may look generic in search results.
kw_in_meta.fix: Weave the primary keyword naturally into your 150–160 character meta description.

kw_in_first_100.meaning: Google reads the opening paragraph first. Mentioning your main keyword early signals that the page truly covers that topic.
kw_in_first_100.ok: Your opening paragraph mentions the main keyword early — good for SEO relevance.
kw_in_first_100.fail: First paragraph doesn't mention what the page is about — weaker topical signal for Google.
kw_in_first_100.fix: Start product description with the product type + key benefit in the first 2 sentences.

content_adequate.meaning: Product pages need enough unique text (typically 300+ words). Very short pages look 'thin' to Google and rank poorly.
content_adequate.ok: Page has sufficient text — Google sees this as a real, useful product page.
content_adequate.fail: Content is too thin (very few words) — Google may treat this as low-quality or duplicate.
content_adequate.fix: Add 300+ words: fabric details, fit guide, styling tips, care instructions, and use cases.

content_unique.meaning: Your description should be original — not copy-pasted from the manufacturer or another website. Duplicate text hurts rankings.
content_unique.ok: Description reads original — not copied from manufacturer or other sites.
content_unique.fail: Content looks generic or duplicated — risk of lower rankings vs competitors.
content_unique.fix: Rewrite description in your brand voice. Add unique selling points only your store offers.

schema_product.meaning: Structured data (JSON-LD code) that tells Google this is a product with price, availability, and reviews — enables star ratings and price in search results.
schema_product.ok: Product JSON-LD is present — Google can show price, rating, and availability in search.
schema_product.fail: No product schema — you miss rich results (stars, price) in Google search.
schema_product.fix: Add Product schema with name, price, availability, image, and brand. Check AutoFix tab for ready code.

tech_canonical.meaning: A canonical tag tells Google: 'This is the official URL for this page.' It prevents duplicate URLs (with tracking params) from splitting your SEO ranking.
tech_canonical.ok: Canonical URL is set — Google knows which version of this page to index.
tech_canonical.fail: No canonical tag — duplicate URLs (with ?utm, filters) may compete and split ranking.
tech_canonical.fix: Add <link rel="canonical" href="YOUR-CLEAN-URL"> in the page <head> pointing to the main product URL.

tech_og.meaning: Open Graph meta tags control how your link looks when shared on WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn — title, image, and description in the preview card.
tech_og.ok: OG tags are set — link previews look good when shared on WhatsApp, Facebook, etc.
tech_og.fail: Missing Open Graph — shared links may show wrong image, title, or no preview.
tech_og.fix: Add og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url meta tags for this product page.

tech_twitter.meaning: Twitter/X card tags ensure your product link shows a rich preview with image and title when posted on X (Twitter).
tech_twitter.ok: Twitter card meta tags present — rich previews when link is posted on X.
tech_twitter.fail: No Twitter card tags — posts may show a plain URL without product image.
tech_twitter.fix: Add twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image meta tags.

tech_hreflang.meaning: Hreflang tags tell Google which language or country version of a page to show (e.g. English-IN vs Arabic-AE). Needed only for multi-region stores.
tech_hreflang.ok: Hreflang tags tell Google which language/region version to show.
tech_hreflang.fail: No hreflang — if you sell in multiple countries/languages, wrong version may rank.
tech_hreflang.fix: Only needed for multi-language stores. Add hreflang links for each locale version of this page.

tech_images_optimized.meaning: Images should be compressed and reasonably sized. Huge image files slow page load — bad for mobile users and Google rankings.
tech_images_optimized.ok: Product images appear reasonably sized — page loads faster.
tech_images_optimized.fail: Very large images detected — slows page load and hurts mobile experience + SEO.
tech_images_optimized.fix: Compress images to WebP, max ~200KB each. Use responsive srcset for different screen sizes.

tech_lazy_loading.meaning: Lazy loading delays loading images until the user scrolls near them — makes the page open faster on mobile.
tech_lazy_loading.ok: Images below the fold load lazily — faster initial page speed.
tech_lazy_loading.fail: All images load at once — slower first paint, especially on mobile.
tech_lazy_loading.fix: Add loading="lazy" to images below the fold (not the main hero product image).

rag_citable.meaning: AI tools (ChatGPT, Perplexity) prefer clear, factual sentences they can quote. Vague marketing copy is harder for AI to cite or recommend.
rag_citable.ok: Clear facts and statements — ChatGPT/Perplexity can quote your page as a source.
rag_citable.fail: Content is vague — AI assistants are less likely to recommend or cite your product.
rag_citable.fix: Add specific facts: material %, weight, dimensions, warranty, origin, and clear product benefits.

rag_uvp.meaning: What makes YOUR product different from every other seller? AI and shoppers need a clear reason to choose you over competitors.
rag_uvp.ok: Page explains why YOUR product is different from generic alternatives.
rag_uvp.fail: Sounds like any other listing — AI and shoppers can't see why to choose you.
rag_uvp.fix: State what makes this product special: patented fabric, brand heritage, exclusive fit, etc.

rag_factual.meaning: Concrete facts (material %, dimensions, warranty, origin) that AI can verify and repeat — not just vague adjectives like 'premium quality'.
rag_factual.ok: Page includes concrete specs and claims AI can verify or repeat.
rag_factual.fail: Mostly marketing fluff — AI engines prefer factual, citable content.
rag_factual.fix: Add measurable details: "98% cotton, 2% elastane", "30-day return", "Made in India".

aeo_conversational.meaning: Content written the way people ask questions — 'Do these shrink after wash?' — performs better in voice search and AI answers.
aeo_conversational.ok: Content answers questions naturally — good for voice search and AI answers.
aeo_conversational.fail: Reads like a spec sheet only — misses how real shoppers ask questions.
aeo_conversational.fix: Add FAQ-style sentences about {product_name} with a direct answer.

aeo_llm_snippet.meaning: A concise 2–3 sentence summary that AI search engines can pull directly into their answers about your product.
aeo_llm_snippet.ok: Content has concise answer blocks AI can pull into responses.
aeo_llm_snippet.fail: No clear snippet-worthy paragraphs — less visibility in AI search (ChatGPT, Gemini).
aeo_llm_snippet.fix: Add a 2–3 sentence summary at top: what it is, who it's for, key benefit.

aeo_not_commodity.meaning: Generic manufacturer copy that every retailer uses makes your page interchangeable. AI treats it as the same product everywhere.
aeo_not_commodity.ok: Content feels specific to this product — not copy-pasted manufacturer text.
aeo_not_commodity.fail: Generic manufacturer copy — AI treats it as interchangeable with every other seller.
aeo_not_commodity.fix: Rewrite with brand story, styling advice, and customer use cases unique to your store.

aeo_unique_perspective.meaning: Your brand's own voice, styling tips, or expert opinion — something only you can say, not copied from a catalog.
aeo_unique_perspective.ok: Your brand's point of view comes through — builds trust and differentiation.
aeo_unique_perspective.fail: No distinct voice — page could belong to any retailer selling the same SKU.
aeo_unique_perspective.fix: Add "Why we love this" section, stylist notes, or brand-specific care tips.

faq_found.meaning: A FAQ section answers common buyer questions on the page itself — helps Google, AI search, and reduces support tickets.
faq_found.ok: FAQs are present — helps AI answers and reduces customer support questions.
faq_found.fail: No FAQ section — missed chance to rank for question searches and AI queries.
faq_found.fix: Add 5–8 FAQs: sizing, shrinkage, returns, styling, fabric care.

faq_conversational.meaning: FAQs written as real questions ('Do these run small?') not corporate jargon ('Size accuracy information').
faq_conversational.ok: Questions written how customers actually ask — better for AI and voice search.
faq_conversational.fail: FAQs are stiff or missing — AI can't match user questions to your content.
faq_conversational.fix: Use real questions: "Do these run true to size?" not "Size accuracy information".

faq_schema.meaning: FAQ structured data code that can make your Q&A appear directly in Google search results as expandable dropdowns.
faq_schema.ok: FAQ structured data added — can show Q&A directly in Google results.
faq_schema.fail: FAQs exist but no schema — Google won't show expandable FAQ in search.
faq_schema.fix: Add FAQPage JSON-LD schema with each question and answer. See AutoFix tab.

speakable_schema.meaning: Markup that tells voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant) which paragraph to read aloud when someone asks about your product.
speakable_schema.ok: Speakable markup helps voice assistants read key content aloud.
speakable_schema.fail: No speakable schema — voice search (Alexa, Google Assistant) may skip your page.
speakable_schema.fix: Add speakable structured data pointing to key summary paragraphs.

cta_found.meaning: A clear Add to Cart or Buy Now button must be visible so visitors know how to purchase.
cta_found.ok: Primary purchase button is present on the page.
cta_found.fail: No clear buy button found — customers may leave without knowing how to purchase.
cta_found.fix: Add a prominent "Add to Cart" or "Buy Now" button near product title and price.

cta_above_fold.meaning: 'Above the fold' means visible on the first screen without scrolling — critical on mobile where most shoppers browse.
cta_above_fold.ok: Buy button visible on first screen — mobile and desktop users can act immediately.
cta_above_fold.fail: Customer must scroll to find buy button — increases drop-off on mobile.
cta_above_fold.fix: Move Add to Cart above the fold on mobile. Keep it visible next to price and size selector.

cta_sticky.meaning: A buy button or bar that stays fixed at the bottom while scrolling — so customers can purchase anytime on long pages.
cta_sticky.ok: Buy button stays visible while scrolling — easy to purchase anytime.
cta_sticky.fail: CTA disappears when scrolling — users on long pages may forget to buy.
cta_sticky.fix: Add a sticky bottom bar on mobile with price + Add to Cart that appears on scroll.

img_angles.meaning: Show the product from front, back, side, and detail shots — online shoppers can't touch the product, so photos must build trust.
img_angles.ok: Several photos show the product from different views — builds confidence.
img_angles.fail: Only one or two images — shoppers can't fully inspect the product online.
img_angles.fix: Add front, back, side, detail (fabric/stitching), and on-model shots — minimum 4–6 images.

img_zoom.meaning: Click or pinch-to-zoom lets customers inspect fabric texture, stitching, and quality up close.
img_zoom.ok: Customers can zoom in to see fabric texture and details.
img_zoom.fail: No zoom — shoppers can't inspect quality, especially for fashion/fabric products.
img_zoom.fix: Enable click-to-zoom or pinch-zoom on product gallery images.

img_lifestyle.meaning: Photos of the product being worn or used in real life — helps customers imagine how it will look on them.
img_lifestyle.ok: Lifestyle photos show how the product looks when worn — helps imagination.
img_lifestyle.fail: Only packshot/flat images — harder for customers to visualize fit and style.
img_lifestyle.fix: Add 2–3 on-model photos in real-life settings (casual, office, etc.).

img_video.meaning: A short product video showing fit, movement, and fabric drape — one of the highest-impact conversion elements on a PDP.
img_video.ok: Video shows product in motion — increases trust and conversion.
img_video.fail: No video — missing a high-impact way to show fit, drape, and quality.
img_video.fix: Add a 15–30 sec clip: model walking, fabric close-up, or 360° spin.

trust_reviews.meaning: Customer reviews are social proof — new visitors trust a product more when they see others have bought and rated it.
trust_reviews.ok: Reviews are shown — social proof helps new buyers decide.
trust_reviews.fail: No reviews visible — new visitors have no proof others bought and liked this.
trust_reviews.fix: Enable product reviews. If new, import/sync reviews or show "Be the first to review".

trust_rating.meaning: Star rating (e.g. 4.5★ from 120 reviews) displayed near the product title — instant trust signal at a glance.
trust_rating.ok: Average rating is visible near product title — quick trust signal.
trust_rating.fail: Rating hidden or missing — shoppers can't gauge quality at a glance.
trust_rating.fix: Show star rating + review count next to product title and price.

trust_return.meaning: Clear return/exchange policy visible on the product page — reduces fear of ordering the wrong size, especially in fashion.
trust_return.ok: Return/exchange policy is easy to find — reduces purchase anxiety.
trust_return.fail: Return policy not visible on PDP — customers worry about being stuck with wrong size.
trust_return.fix: Add "Easy 30-day returns" link or badge near Add to Cart button.

trust_security.meaning: SSL lock, secure payment icons, COD/UPI badges near checkout — reassures customers their payment is safe.
trust_security.ok: Secure checkout badges visible — reassures customers about payment safety.
trust_security.fail: No trust badges — some customers hesitate to enter card details.
trust_security.fix: Show SSL, secure payment icons, COD option, or UPI badges near checkout CTA.

info_size_guide.meaning: A size chart with measurements (chest, waist, length) helps customers pick the right size and reduces returns.
info_size_guide.ok: Size guide helps customers pick the right fit — fewer returns.
info_size_guide.fail: No size guide — fashion PDPs without sizing info have high return rates.
info_size_guide.fix: Add size chart popup with measurements in inches/cm and fit tips.

info_material.meaning: Fabric composition (e.g. 98% Cotton, 2% Elastane) and care instructions — essential for fashion and home products.
info_material.ok: Fabric breakdown is shown — customers know what they're buying.
info_material.fail: Material info missing — shoppers can't judge comfort, care, or quality.
info_material.fix: List fabric composition: "98% Cotton, 2% Elastane" plus care instructions.

info_fit.meaning: Explains whether the product is slim, regular, or relaxed fit — and whether to size up or down.
info_fit.ok: Fit type explained (slim, regular, relaxed) — helps size decisions.
info_fit.fail: No fit guidance — customers guess size and often order wrong.
info_fit.fix: State fit type and advice: "Slim fit — size up if you prefer relaxed fit."

info_specs.meaning: A structured table of key specs (rise, closure, pockets, care) — easier to scan than buried paragraphs.
info_specs.ok: Structured specs table makes details easy to scan and compare.
info_specs.fail: No specs table — key details buried in paragraphs or missing.
info_specs.fix: Add a table: Rise, Fit, Closure, Pockets, Care, Country of origin.

urgency_stock.meaning: 'Only 3 left in stock' creates urgency — but must be real inventory, never fake.
urgency_stock.ok: Low stock message creates urgency — can boost conversion.
urgency_stock.fail: No stock signal — missed urgency trigger (optional but helpful).
urgency_stock.fix: Show "Only 3 left" when stock is low. Don't fake it — use real inventory.

urgency_limited.meaning: A countdown or deadline ('Offer ends Sunday') encourages faster purchase decisions during sales.
urgency_limited.ok: Time-bound offer visible — encourages faster purchase decision.
urgency_limited.fail: No time-limited offer shown — optional nudge for conversion.
urgency_limited.fix: If running a sale, show countdown or "Offer ends Sunday" near price.

urgency_social.meaning: '12 people viewing this' or '48 sold today' — live activity signals that others trust and buy this product.
urgency_social.ok: "X people viewing" or "Y sold today" builds FOMO and trust.
urgency_social.fail: No live social proof — page feels static vs competitors who show activity.
urgency_social.fix: Add "12 people viewing this" or "48 sold in last 24 hours" if your platform supports it.

checkout_guest.meaning: Allowing purchase without forced account signup — many shoppers abandon cart if they must register first.
checkout_guest.ok: Customers can buy without forced account creation — lower friction.
checkout_guest.fail: Forced signup implied — many shoppers abandon at checkout.
checkout_guest.fix: Offer "Checkout as Guest" option. Collect email only for order tracking.

checkout_one_click.meaning: Express payment options (UPI, Apple Pay, Shop Pay) that complete purchase in one tap — fewer steps = more sales.
checkout_one_click.ok: Express checkout (Shop Pay, UPI, etc.) reduces steps to purchase.
checkout_one_click.fail: Multi-step checkout only — every extra click loses customers.
checkout_one_click.fix: Add Shop Pay, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or UPI one-tap where possible.

tech_mobile.meaning: Your page works well on phones. Google uses mobile-first indexing, so a bad mobile page hurts rankings.
tech_mobile.ok: Page is usable on mobile — good for Google rankings and phone shoppers.
tech_mobile.fail: Mobile experience is weak — you lose rankings and phone sales.
tech_mobile.fix: Use responsive theme, large tap targets, readable font size, and test on a real phone.

tech_pagination.meaning: Pagination splits products across pages. Wrong setup can confuse Google and create duplicate content.
tech_pagination.ok: Pagination is set up correctly or not needed on this product page.
tech_pagination.fail: Pagination may confuse search engines or create duplicate URLs.
tech_pagination.fix: Use canonical tags; product pages should have one clear canonical URL.

geo_perplexity.meaning: Perplexity is an AI search engine. Your page needs clear facts and FAQs to get cited.
geo_perplexity.ok: Content is structured so Perplexity can quote your product.
geo_perplexity.fail: Page is too generic — Perplexity will cite competitors instead.
geo_perplexity.fix: Add specific FAQs, specs, and unique selling points in plain sentences.

geo_sge.meaning: Google AI Overviews show AI answers at the top of search. Clear answers and schema help you get featured.
geo_sge.ok: Page format fits Google AI Overview snippets.
geo_sge.fail: Page may not qualify for AI Overview inclusion.
geo_sge.fix: Add FAQ schema and direct answers in H2 sections.

geo_direct_answer.meaning: Content answers questions in the first 1–2 sentences. AI engines prefer this format.
geo_direct_answer.ok: Key questions are answered upfront in clear sentences.
geo_direct_answer.fail: Answers are buried in fluff — AI skips your page.
geo_direct_answer.fix: Start each section with a direct answer, then add detail.

schema_breadcrumb.meaning: Breadcrumb shows path like Home › Category › Product. Schema helps Google display it in search.
schema_breadcrumb.ok: Breadcrumb schema helps Google show your category path.
schema_breadcrumb.fail: No breadcrumb schema — missed context in search.
schema_breadcrumb.fix: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD matching your visible breadcrumb.

schema_review.meaning: Structured data for star ratings — can show stars in Google results.
schema_review.ok: Review schema may enable star ratings in search.
schema_review.fail: No review schema — stars won't show in Google.
schema_review.fix: Add AggregateRating schema with real review count and rating.

price_charm.meaning: Prices ending in 9 or 99 feel like a better deal (₹999 vs ₹1000).
price_charm.ok: Charm pricing can lift perceived value.
price_charm.fail: Round pricing only — small psychological lift missed.
price_charm.fix: Consider ₹1,999 instead of ₹2,000 if it fits your brand.

price_anchor.meaning: Showing original price crossed out next to sale price makes the deal feel bigger.
price_anchor.ok: Anchor price makes discount feel significant.
price_anchor.fail: No compare-at price — discount feels weaker.
price_anchor.fix: Show MRP struck through next to sale price when on offer.

trust_moneyback.meaning: Clear refund guarantee reduces purchase fear.
trust_moneyback.ok: Guarantee visible — builds confidence to buy.
trust_moneyback.fail: No guarantee messaging — shoppers worry about returns.
trust_moneyback.fix: Add 30-day easy returns or money-back guarantee near Add to Cart.

emotion_identity.meaning: Copy speaks to who the customer is, not just what the product is.
emotion_identity.ok: Customer sees themselves in your message.
emotion_identity.fix: Add who this product is for and what lifestyle it fits.

emotion_aspirational.meaning: Words that paint the life the customer wants, not only specs.
emotion_aspirational.fix: Add how they will feel using the product.

decoy_pricing.meaning: Three options where the middle looks like best value guides shoppers.
decoy_pricing.ok: Tiered options guide shoppers to preferred SKU.
decoy_pricing.fail: Single option only — no anchoring between variants.
decoy_pricing.fix: Highlight a recommended variant as Best value.

peak_end_rule.meaning: People remember the last thing they read. Strong FAQ or CTA at bottom helps conversion.
peak_end_rule.ok: Page ends with trust or action.
peak_end_rule.fix: End with FAQ, guarantee, or strong CTA above footer.
